title: andira inermis subsp. grandiflora
intro: Andira inermis subsp. grandiflora, also known as the 'Pink Andiroba', is a subspecies of the Andira inermis tree, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, making it a popular ingredient in traditional herbal remedies. The health benefits of Andira inermis subsp. grandiflora include its potential to treat skin conditions, such as acne and wounds, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and improve immune function. In herbal preparations, the bark and seeds of the plant are commonly used to make teas, infusions, and ointments, which are applied topically to the skin or taken internally to promote overall health and well-being.
